response:
I don't have file-write tools available in this session (only ToolSearch, Workflow, ScheduleWakeup, ReportFindings, and a few deferred integration tools — no Bash/Write/Edit), so I can't create `outputs/generating-release-notes/SKILL.md` on disk. I've written the full skill content below for you to save at that path.

One thing before the content: I cut three parts of the doc rather than relocating them — the Background section (semver/git/release-notes definitions), the History of This Process section, and the "ABSOLUTELY AMAZING!!!" line. Skill bodies are loaded into every relevant conversation, so their cost is per-use, not one-time — background Claude already knows and process backstory (Confluence era, the Q3 2025 script, Priya's rewrite) don't change how the skill should be *run*, and the marketing line has zero instructional content. This follows this repo's own skill-authoring rule ("cut means delete, not relocate") and the muda guidance against git-tellable backstory in docs. The functional content — the two-command lookup, the full type mapping including the `!` and security-scope special cases, and every output rule — is preserved in full below.

```markdown
---
name: generating-release-notes
description: Use when generating release notes for a new release from git commit history, or when asked to turn commits since the last tag into a changelog. Groups conventional commits by type and writes RELEASE_NOTES.md.
---

## Generate release notes

1. Find the last release tag: `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
2. Collect commits since then: `git log <tag>..HEAD --oneline`
3. Group commits by their conventional-commit type prefix into sections (below).

### Type -> section mapping

| Prefix | Section |
|---|---|
| any type with `!` (e.g. `feat!`) | Breaking Changes — always the FIRST section |
| `fix(security)` or any security scope | Security — always before Bug Fixes |
| `feat` | Features |
| `fix` | Bug Fixes |
| `perf` | Performance |
| `revert` | Reverts — also remove the reverted commit's own entry if it's in the same release |
| `refactor` | omit unless user-facing |
| `docs`, `chore`, `ci`, `build` | omit |

### Output

Write markdown to `RELEASE_NOTES.md` at the repo root.

- Section order: Breaking Changes, Security, Features, Performance, Bug Fixes, Reverts. Omit empty sections.
- Each entry: one line, imperative mood, no trailing period, PR linked as `[#123]`.

Example:

```markdown
## Breaking Changes
- Remove deprecated v1 auth endpoint [#412]

## Security
- Patch token leak in session refresh [#418]

## Features
- Add bulk export for reports [#405]

## Bug Fixes
- Fix off-by-one in pagination cursor [#409]
```
```

Want me to also draft an `evals/evals.json` with a few test cases (e.g., a commit range with a `feat!`, a `fix(security)`, and a same-release revert) per the skill-creation framework's "draft evaluations first" step?
